title: Campaign Manager Path
subtitle: A systems-level leadership pathway for designing, staffing, and executing campaigns.
description: Build the full civic and operational stack needed to lead local or statewide campaigns in Arkansas, including strategy, narrative, field systems, rule changes, and long-term sustainability.
audience: Campaign managers, field directors, chiefs of staff, organizers moving into leadership.

milestones.title: Context and Terrain
milestones.description: Complete the context and terrain milestone by finishing the required Arkansas civics modules.

course_refs.reason: Ground the learner in Arkansas civic realities.
Add the historical context behind present strategy.

milestones.title: Campaign Systems
milestones.description: Complete the campaign systems milestone by finishing the required Arkansas civics modules.

course_refs.reason: Learn issue selection, county strategy, petition systems, and campaign buildout.
Develop message architecture and media discipline.

milestones.title: Opposition and Adaptation
milestones.description: Complete the opposition and adaptation milestone by finishing the required Arkansas civics modules.

course_refs.reason: Understand backlash, legal gatekeeping, and movement adaptation.
Translate strategy into scalable organizing systems.

milestones.title: Sustaining the Machine
milestones.description: Complete the sustaining the machine milestone by finishing the required Arkansas civics modules.

course_refs.reason: Build durable infrastructure, replication, and long-tail systems.
